name: The Camp de Morvedre — Circular from Sagunto
name_original: El Camp de Morvedre — Circular desde Sagunto
region_intro: Road cycling around Rafelbunyol offers diverse landscapes, from smooth, paved routes ideal for speed to more challenging segments with varied elevation. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Barranc del Carraixet, providing routes that blend urban edges with countryside feelings, and the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, which features abundant Mediterranean forests and accessible climbs. Cyclists can find routes on mostly paved surfaces, suitable for various fitness levels, alongside options that include unpaved sections and steeper gradients. Best road cycling routes around Rafelbunyol The most popular road cycling route is The Camp de Morvedre — Circular from Sagunto, a 41.6 miles (66.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 28 minutes to complete, offering varied terrain around Sagunto. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Aín Village – Coll d’Aín loop from La Pobla de Farnals, a difficult 72.9 miles (117.3 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, leading through the scenic Sierra Calderona. Local road cyclists also love the Clock Building – Terraced Citrus Orchard loop from El Puig, a 47.7 miles (76.8 km) trail leading through coastal areas and agricultural landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Road cycling around Rafelbunyol is defined by smooth routes, the Barranc del Carraixet ravine, and the mountainous terrain of the Sierra Calderona. The network offers options for different ability levels, from flat sections to challenging climbs. The routes in Rafelbunyol are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 140 reviews. More than 11,800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Rafelbunyol's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Rafelbunyol?
A: There are over 130 road cycling routes around Rafelbunyol, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these routes over 11,800 times.

Q: What are the general characteristics of the road cycling terrain in the Rafelbunyol region?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from smooth, paved sections ideal for speed to more challenging segments with varied elevation. You'll find routes that blend urban edges with countryside feelings, particularly along the Barranc del Carraixet, and mountainous terrain in the nearby Sierra Calderona Natural Park.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those seeking easier rides?
A: Yes, Rafelbunyol offers 37 easy road cycling routes. These typically feature mostly paved surfaces and gentler gradients, making them accessible for various fitness levels. Look for routes with lower elevation gain for a more relaxed experience.

Q: Where can I find more challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: For more challenging rides, head towards the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39565648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aín Village – Coll d’Aín loop from La Pobla de Farnals&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 72.9 miles (117.3 km) with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain, offer demanding ramps amidst scenic landscapes. There are 30 difficult routes in the area for experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes around Rafelbunyol are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e859627051/the-camp-de-morvedre-circular-from-sagunto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Camp de Morvedre — Circular from Sagunto&lt;/a&gt;, a 41.6-mile (66.9 km) route offering varied terrain.

Q: What natural features or scenic views can I expect to see while cycling?
A: You can expect beautiful Valencian landscapes, including pine, olive, and orange groves. The Barranc del Carraixet ravine offers a countryside feel, while the Sierra Calderona Natural Park provides stunning Mediterranean forests. You might also encounter wetlands like the Marjal dels Moros. A notable cycling attraction is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5040812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Footbridge over the Barranc del Carraixet&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, the region boasts historical attractions such as Sagunto Castle, which can be incorporated into longer cycling routes. While cycling, you might also pass through charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2780114" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port Saplaya&lt;/a&gt;, known for its coastal beauty.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Rafelbunyol?
A: The road cycling routes around Rafelbunyol are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 140 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the mix of smooth and challenging terrains, and the accessible climbs, especially in the Sierra Calderona.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Rafelbunyol?
A: Rafelbunyol generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. The region's Mediterranean climate means pleasant temperatures outside of the hottest summer months. Spring and autumn are often ideal, providing comfortable weather for exploring the diverse routes.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Rafelbunyol?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' road cycling routes are not explicitly categorized, the 37 easy routes available, often on mostly paved surfaces with gentler gradients, can be suitable for families with older children or those looking for a more relaxed ride. Always check the route profile for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: Is parking available near the starting points of road cycling routes?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Rafelbunyol and the starting points of many routes. For routes beginning in towns like Sagunto or La Pobla de Farnals, you can typically find public parking facilities. It's advisable to check local parking regulations before your ride.

Q: Can I find places to stop for refreshments or food along the routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near towns and villages where you can find cafes, restaurants, and shops for refreshments. For example, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39544669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bar El Pastoret – Old Civil War Airfield loop from La Pobla de Farnals&lt;/a&gt; will often have opportunities to stop in local establishments.

Q: How can I access the road cycling routes using public transport?
A: Rafelbunyol is part of the Valencia metropolitan area, which has public transport options. You can often reach starting points of routes via local train or bus services, though specific bike carriage policies may vary. It's recommended to check the local public transport provider's website for details on bringing bicycles.
tips: Whether ascending from the N-234 or descending from El Garbi, this section is truly amazing. The views along the way are amazing and even more so when you reach the top of Garbi.
A classic of the Sierra Calderona. Promoted in numerous editions of the Tour of Spain. On weekends it is a cycling show!

waypoints.name: Descent from Garbi, through Segart down to the N234
